description:
Offered: every Spring
Students will engage in a hands-on study of the methods and practices involved in the research and development of documentary films and other non-fiction media, across a variety of representational styles and formats. Students will uncover a topic of interest and immerse in the conception, planning, research, and writing stages that precede the production phase of non-fiction filmmaking and other forms of the genre.
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200 or HON 1200 and Junior or Senior standing.
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, IHIP
prerequisites: ENGL 1200 or HON 1200 and Junior or Senior standing.
